content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 25, 2007

Stuart Waldman                                                     VIA FACSIMILE and U.S. MAIL
P.O. Box 5628
Sherman Oaks, CA 91413

RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
      Our File No. I-07-073

Dear Mr. Waldman:

        This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding your responsibilities
under Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your request is
general in nature and does not involve a specific action pending before the agency on
which you serve, we treat your request as one for informal assistance.²

QUESTION

        Does Section 84308 apply to you as a member of the California Board of
Accountancy ("CBA")? If so, what restrictions and/or requirements would apply to you
and to contributions for your Assembly campaign?

CONCLUSION

        Section 84308 applies to you as a board member of the CBA. Therefore, you may
not accept, solicit or direct a contribution of more than $250 for your Assembly campaign
if the contributor is involved in a decision regarding a license, permit or other entitlement
for use that is pending before the CBA and for three months following the date of CBA's
final decision on the matter. In addition, if you receive a contribution from a party to or
participant in the proceeding of more than $250 within the 12 months prior to rendering a
decision in the proceeding, you must: (1) disclose receipt of the contribution on the
record of the proceeding and disqualify yourself from the decision, or (2) return the
contribution within 30 days from the time you know or should have known about the
contribution and the proceeding. If you return the contribution (or that portion of the
_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear in Title 2, Sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² Please note that informal assistance does not provide the requestor with immunity provided by
an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3), enclosed.)

File No. I-07-073
Page No. 2

contribution that is more than $250) within the specified time, then disqualification is not
required.
FACTS

You are a candidate for California State Assembly, and you currently serve as a
board member on the CBA. According to the CBA website, the board consists of 15-
members, all of whom are appointed either by the Governor, the Senate Rules
Committee, or the Speaker of the Assembly. You were appointed to the board by
Assembly Speaker Fabian Nunez.

The CBA, which was created by statute in 1901, is a state agency that regulates
more than 75,000 licensees in the accounting profession. These licensees consist of
Certified Public Accountants ("CPAs") and Public Accountants (PAs). The CBA also
qualifies state candidates for the National Uniform CPA Examination; certifies, licenses,
and renews licenses of individual CPAs and PAs; registers CPA partnerships, PA
partnerships and corporations. In addition, the CBA receives and investigates and takes
enforcement action on complaints against licensees for violations of CBA statutes and
regulations; monitors compliance with continuing education requirements; and reviews
work product of CPAs, PAs and CPA firms to ensure adherence to professional
standards. The CBA is self-funded, supported by fees imposed upon the licensees it
regulates. It does not draw money from taxpayers or the state's General Fund.

You wish to know if Section 84308 applies to you as a member of the CBA, and
if so, what requirements would you be subject to in soliciting contributions for your
Assembly campaign.

ANALYSIS

Section 84308 covers campaign contributions and governmental decision-making
by officials serving on appointive public agencies. This law applies to appointed board
members, or other individuals who serve on state or local government
agencies, as defined in Section 84308(a)(3), and who make decisions in proceedings that
involve licenses, permits, or other entitlements for use.

Who is covered?

Section 84308(a)(3) defines "agency" as any state agency or local government
agency "except that it does not include the courts or any agency in the judicial branch of
government, local governmental agencies whose members are directly elected by the
voters, the Legislature, the Board of Equalization, or constitutional officers." ³

³ Section 84308 references Section 82003, which defines "agency" to include any state or local
government agency.

File No. I-07-073
Page No. 3

Section 84308(a)(4) defines "officer" as any elected or appointed officer of an
agency, any alternate to an elected or appointed officer of an agency, and any candidate
for elective office in an agency.

The CBA is a state agency included under the coverage of Section 84308, and you
are an appointed officer of this agency. Therefore, Section 84308 applies to you as a
board member of the CBA.

When are contributions prohibited?

Section 84308(b) provides that you may not accept, solicit, or direct a
contribution of more than $250 from a contributor who is a party to or participant in a
pending decision before your agency until three months after the decision is final.

In addition, Section 84308(c) states that if you receive a contribution from a party
to or participant in the proceeding of more than $250 within the 12 months prior to
rendering a decision in the proceeding, you must: (1) disclose receipt of the contribution
on the record of the proceeding and disqualify yourself from the decision, or (2) return
the contribution within 30 days from the time you know or should have known about the
contribution and the proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use.
If you return the contribution (or that portion of the contribution that is more than $250)
within the specified time, then disqualification is not required.⁴

Section 84308's restrictions on officials accepting, soliciting, or directing
contributions from persons with pending proceedings before a state agency require a fact
specific analysis. You have not provided us with sufficient facts to make a determination
regarding whether any particular contribution would require your disqualification with
regard to a specific decision before your agency. Therefore, we can only provide you
with general guidance in this area.

We have enclosed the Commission's fact sheet entitled, "Campaign Contributions
May Cause Conflicts for Appointees and Commissioners," for your review. We also
encourage you to contact us for future advice as specific situations arise.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures

⁴ Regulation 18438.7, enclosed, discusses an official's knowledge of pending proceedings, parties
and participants to the proceeding, and their contributions.
